title: selinum austriacum var. montanum
intro: Selinum austriacum var. montanum, commonly known as wild carrot or Queen Anne's lace, is a flowering plant in the carrot family that belongs to the genus Selinum. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic, carminative, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Selinum austriacum var. montanum include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of Selinum austriacum var. montanum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
